cover.title: The Complete China Guide
cover.subtitle: Choose your journey for a tailored guide

meta.title: Visa
meta.sub: Select your nationality to see applicable policies.

s1.kicker: Find Out If You Need One

meta.title: Preparation
meta.sub: Checklist + arrival card + final check. 97% Beijing arrivals use online filing.

s2.kicker: What to Sort Before You Fly
s2.intro: Before you fly, make sure you have these essentials ready. Tap each item for details.

passport.label: Passport
passport.title: Passport Requirements
steps: Valid for at least 6 months beyond your planned departure from China.
At least 2 blank visa pages (amendment/endorsement pages do not count).
If your passport expires soon: renew it before applying for a Chinese visa.
Keep a digital copy (photo or scan) of your passport ID page on your phone and in email — in case of loss.
For dual citizens: use the same passport throughout (visa application, entry, exit).

tickets.label: Return+Hotel
tickets.title: Return Ticket & Hotel Booking
steps: Print or screenshot your return/onward flight ticket — immigration may ask to see proof of departure.
Hotel booking confirmation with full address in Chinese and English.
If staying with friends/family: have an invitation letter with the host's name, address, phone number, and ID copy.
Pro tip: book a refundable hotel for visa/entry purposes, then cancel after arrival if plans change.

arrivalCard.label: Arrival Card
arrivalCard.title: Arrival Card — Pre-fill Online
steps: Visit s.nia.gov.cn before your flight — fill out the arrival card online (free).
Take a screenshot of the QR code after submission — you will scan this at the immigration counter.
The form supports passport photo scan (auto-fills your details).
Alternative methods: NIA 12367 APP (iOS/Android), WeChat mini-program, Alipay mini-program.
97% of travelers at Beijing Capital Airport now use the online form. Skip the paper form entirely.
Each traveler needs their own arrival card (including children).

finalCheck.label: Final Check
finalCheck.title: Final Checklist Before Departure
steps: Passport: valid for 6+ months, 2+ blank pages.
Visa or visa-free eligibility confirmed (use S1 to check).
Arrival card: filled online, QR code screenshot saved on phone.
Alipay / WeChat: downloaded and foreign card linked (do this before departure — see S5).
Hotel address: saved in Chinese and English on your phone (show to immigration and taxi driver).
VPN: installed, paid, and tested before departure (see S4).
Travel insurance: policy number saved, emergency contact number noted.

steps.title: Immigration — 6 Steps
steps: Health Check: Temperature scan. Show health declaration QR.
Fingerprint: All foreigners 14-70. Under 14 / over 70 exempt.
Border Control: Passport + visa + arrival card. Photo taken.
Baggage Claim: Check screens for carousel number.
Customs: Green (nothing to declare) or Red channel.
Arrival Hall: Taxi, metro, airport bus, ride-hailing.

customs.title: Customs Rules
steps: Cash: Up to $5,000 USD. Declare if exceeding.
Alcohol: 1.5L total.
Cigarettes: 400 sticks (2 cartons).
Electronics: Personal fine. Gifts >5000Y should declare.
Rx Medication: Doctor note + prescription required.
Prohibited: Weapons, drugs, ivory, fresh fruit/plants.
Green Channel: Nothing to declare.
Red Channel: Goods exceed limits. Fill declaration form.

registration.title: Accommodation Registration
steps: Hotels: Automatic at check-in with passport.
Private: Go to police station within 24h.
Online (7 cities): Shanghai, Beijing, Guangzhou, Shenzhen, Chengdu, Hangzhou, Xiamen.
Legal: Exit-Entry Law Art.39 — mandatory.
Re-register when moving address/city.
12367 hotline: 24/7 multi-language.

s3.kicker: From Tarmac to Taxi in 6 Steps
s3.intro: Landing in China? Tap to learn about immigration, customs, and registration.

esim.title: eSIM — Buy Before You Fly
steps: Airalo: China from $5 (1GB/7 days).
Holafly: Unlimited data, China from $19.
Nomad: Budget, China from $4.
Buy & install BEFORE departure.
Note: Data only, no phone number.

sim.title: China SIM Card
steps: Airport arrival hall kiosks (Mobile/Unicom/Telecom).
Need: Original passport.
Plans: 29-99Y/month. Data + calls + SMS.
Get number -> register Alipay/WeChat/DiDi.
Keep home SIM for SMS verification.

vpn.title: VPN — Install Before Arriving
steps: Mullvad: 5EUR/month. Open-source. WireGuard.
Astrill: $15-30/month. China-optimized.
Install + pay BEFORE arriving.
Keep 2 protocols: WireGuard + fallback.
Test before departure.

blocked.title: Blocked Services & Alternatives
steps: Blocked: Google, Gmail, Maps, YT, WA, IG, FB, X, Dropbox, LINE.
Works: iCloud, Slack, Zoom, Teams, Outlook.
Maps -> Amap/Baidu Maps.
Instagram -> Xiaohongshu.
Twitter/X -> Weibo.
YouTube -> Bilibili.
WhatsApp -> WeChat.

s4.intro: Google, WhatsApp, Instagram blocked. Get connected before you land.

meta.title: Payment
meta.sub: China is cashless. Link foreign card before — most critical step.

s5.intro: China is largely cashless — mobile payment is the default, accounting for over 86% of consumer transactions. Foreign visitors can now link Visa, Mastercard, JCB, and Amex directly to Alipay and WeChat Pay. No Chinese bank account needed.
s5.backup: 💡 BackupCarry ¥500-1,000 RMB cash as backup. ATMs at Bank of China / ICBC / China Merchants Bank accept foreign cards (look for Visa/MC logo). If one payment app fails, try the other.

alipay.title: Alipay Setup Guide
steps: Download Alipay from alipay.com or your app store
Sign up with your phone number → verify identity with passport photo
Me → Bank Cards → Add Card → Visa / Mastercard / JCB / Amex supported
Scan merchant QR or show your pay code. ≤¥200/transaction: no fee. >¥200: 3% service fee.
TourPass: prepaid card for visitors — no Chinese bank account needed

wechat.title: WeChat Pay Setup Guide
steps: Download WeChat from wechat.com or your app store
Me → Services → Wallet → Cards → Add foreign Visa/MC/Amex
Link card (lower spending limit than Alipay; some features require Chinese bank card)
Mini-Programs: order food, hail taxis, buy train tickets — all inside WeChat

s6.intro: Getting around, eating well, and finding a place to stay. Tap each item for practical tips.

transport.title: Getting Around
steps: DiDi: Ride-hailing built into Alipay. English UI. Pay with linked foreign card.
Metro: Use Alipay transport QR code at gates. 40+ cities. No ticket machine needed.
HSR (High-Speed Rail): Book at 12306.cn/en. Foreign cards accepted.
Shared Bikes: Scan with Alipay/Meituan. 1.5-3Y/ride. No deposit for most.
Tip: Alipay mini-programs handle DiDi, metro, bikes, trains — one app.

food.title: Dining Out
steps: Dianping: Restaurant reviews, photos, ratings. Search by area or cuisine.
WeChat Scan > Translate: Point camera at Chinese menu, auto-translates.
Meituan / Ele.me: Food delivery to your hotel (like Uber Eats).
Halal: Look for halal sign or Lanzhou beef noodle chains.
Vegetarian: Say chun su (pure vegan). Watch for meat broth.
Allergy: Carry a bilingual allergy card. Show to staff.

accommodation.title: Where to Stay
steps: Trip.com / Booking.com: Filter accepts foreign guests. Not all hotels can host foreigners.
Airbnb: Stopped China operations. Use Tujia instead.
Foreign Guest Qualification: Chinese hotels need a special license. Budget hotels may turn you away.
Foreigner-friendly chains: Marriott, Hilton, IHG, Hyatt, Jin Jiang.
If turned away: Call 12367 for help finding compliant accommodation.

clothing.title: What to Wear
steps: Sizing: China sizes 1-2 smaller than US/EU. US M = CN L/XL.
Spring (Mar-May): 10-22C. Light jacket + layers.
Summer (Jun-Aug): 28-38C hot humid. Breathable fabrics, sunscreen, umbrella.
Autumn (Sep-Nov): 10-25C. Best season. Light sweater.
Winter (Dec-Feb): North -10C heavy coat. South 0-10C damp cold, layers work best.

meta.title: Medical
meta.sub: 850+ institutions across 57 cities. Tap cards for details.

hospital.title: 🏥 International Medical Services
hospital.intro: 850+ institutions across 57 cities. 1.28M international patients in 2025 (+73.6%).
hospital.insuranceDetail: Outpatient ¥800-1,500+. Intl dept registration ¥600-1,200. Recommend Cigna/Allianz/Bupa.

big3.title: 🦷👁️🌿 Medical "Big Three"
big3.dentalNote: 💡 Cost advantage: China dental 25-35% of US/EU. Source: Dianping, Reddit expat feedback (2024-2025)
big3.dentalClinicsDetail: Jiahui Dental Shanghai (English)·UFH Dental Beijing·iKang Dental Shenzhen·Arrail Dental (Nationwide). Tip: Book ahead, ask about English service.
big3.eyeDesc: Glasses same-day service (¥500-2,000). Laser eye surgery ¥8K-20K. Recommended: Shanghai Eye & ENT Hospital, Beijing Tongren, Aier Eye Hospital (Nationwide).
big3.tcmHotspotsDetail: Sanya TCM Hospital(intl)·Heihe TCM(RU service,2000+ patients/yr)·Hunchun Hospital(RU interpreter)·Raohe TCM(spa+massage)·Tongrentang Beijing(traditional)

checkup.title: 📋 Health Checkup
checkup.intro: Major hospitals offer executive checkups, ¥2K-10K+. Some 24h reports. No overnight stay, half-day visit.
checkup.recommended: Recommended: PUMCH Intl, Huashan Intl, Jiahui Intl
checkup.warningDetail: Book 1-2 weeks ahead. Fasting required for some tests. Consult doctor about medications.

s7.kicker: World-Class Care, Fraction of the Cost

meta.title: Shopping
meta.sub: Tax refund travelers +305% in 2025. 12,252 stores, 7,000+ instant refund.

electronics.title: 📱 Electronics & Gadgets
electronics.whereToBuyDetail: SZ Huaqiangbei—"China Electronics Street",35 markets,multilingual+cards+tax refund. BJ Wangfujing—flagship stores. Yiwu—"World Supermarket",one-stop.

souvenirs.title: 🎨 Cultural Souvenirs
souvenirs.tip: 💡 Cultural shops cluster near scenic spots and heritage streets

taxrefund.title: 💰 Tax Refund Guide
taxrefund.stats: 📊 12,252 refund stores nationwide. +305% refund travelers in 2025.
taxrefund.instantRefundDetail: 7,000+ stores: deduct at purchase, no airport queue. Shanghai city-wide. Hainan duty-free.

shenzhen.name: Shenzhen·Huaqiangbei
shenzhen.subtitle: China Electronics Street
shenzhen.desc: "China Electronics Street",35 specialized markets. Categories: phones, accessories, drones, wearables. Multilingual signs+intl cards+tax refund. Metro Line 1 Huaqiang Rd. Tip: compare prices, some markets negotiable.

shanghai.name: Shanghai·Fabric Market
shanghai.subtitle: Qipao & Suit Tailoring
shanghai.desc: South Bund Fabric Market etc. Custom qipao & suits, measured on-site. ¥300-2,000+, 2-3 day turnaround. Intl cards accepted.

yiwu.name: Yiwu·Trade City
yiwu.subtitle: World Supermarket
yiwu.desc: "World Supermarket", phone cases to smart appliances to drones. Flight bookings +60% H1 2026. One-stop shopping, multilingual+intl cards.

sanya.name: Sanya·Duty Free Mall
sanya.subtitle: Offshore Duty-Free
sanya.desc: Offshore & departure duty-free. Full range of intl brands. Policy upgraded 2025. Simple process: register→scan→pay→airport pickup, <3min.

beijing.name: Beijing·Wangfujing
beijing.subtitle: Flagship·Heritage
beijing.desc: Flagship electronics stores, heritage brands+modern malls. Same-day glasses. Dense tax refund stores with instant refund.

s8.kicker: Buy the Best + Get Your Tax Back

meta.title: Destinations
meta.sub: Foreign travelers have explored nearly 500 cities. Tap to discover.

seasons.title: 🌍 Seasonal Picks

spring.title: 🌸 Spring (Mar-May)
spring.desc: Mild & pleasant. Hangzhou West Lake·Suzhou Gardens·Kunming flowers.

summer.title: 🌿 Summer (Jun-Aug)
summer.desc: Escape heat: Beidaihe/Qingdao. Beer Festival·Harbin cool·Zhangjiajie.

autumn.title: 🍂 Autumn (Sep-Nov) Best!
autumn.desc: Best nationwide. Beijing red leaves·Xi'an ancient city·Guilin scenery.

winter.title: ❄️ Winter (Dec-Feb)
winter.desc: North ice vs South sun. Harbin Ice Festival(SE Asia fav)·Sanya tropics(Russian fav).

seasons.sizingTip: 👔 Sizing: CN 1-2 smaller than US. US M≈CN L/XL. Uniqlo/ZARA global sizing.

s10.kicker: When Things Go Wrong — Who to Call
s10.intro: Save these numbers. Tap each one to learn when to call and what to expect.
s10.departIntro: Leaving China? Here is your pre-flight checklist.
s10.passportWarningDetail: 1) File a police report immediately (call 110). 2) Contact your embassy/consulate for an emergency travel document. 3) Keep a photo of your passport ID page and visa on your phone.

12367.label: Immigration 24h
12367.title: 12367 — NIA Immigration Hotline
steps: National Immigration Administration 24/7 hotline.
Languages: Chinese, English, Russian, Japanese, Korean, and more.
When to call: Visa questions, registration, overstay, lost passport, entry/exit inquiries.
Also as mobile APP and WeChat mini-program.
From abroad: +86-10-12367.

110.label: Police
110.title: 110 — Police
steps: Emergency police number (like 911/112).
When to call: Crime, theft, lost passport, safety threat, traffic accident.
State your location and need for English-speaking assistance.
Non-emergency: visit nearest police station for reports.
After reporting lost passport, get a written police report receipt.

120.label: Ambulance
120.title: 120 — Medical Emergency
steps: Emergency medical services.
When to call: Serious injury, chest pain, breathing difficulty, severe bleeding, unconsciousness.
Ambulance arrives 10-15 min in cities. Paramedics may have limited English.
Have ready: location, passport, insurance card, medications list.
If possible, go directly to an international hospital (see S7).

119.label: Fire
119.title: 119 — Fire Emergency
steps: Fire and rescue services.
When to call: Fire, gas leak, trapped in elevator, building collapse, flood.
Give address, what is burning, if anyone is trapped.
Note fire exit map on hotel room door.

depart.label: Depart
depart.title: Departure Checklist
steps: Arrive 2-3 hours early for international check-in, security, tax refund.
Carry tax refund items in hand luggage. Get customs stamp before checking bags.
Spend or convert leftover RMB at airport.
Backup photos to iCloud or USB before leaving (Google Photos blocked).
Return portable WiFi / collect SIM deposits.
Keep arrival card stub — may need at exit border control.

beijing.label: Beijing
beijing.desc: Ancient capital. Must-see: Forbidden City, Great Wall, Temple of Heaven. Food: Peking duck, zhajiang noodles, hotpot lamb. Shop: Wangfujing, Panjiayuan antiques. Tip: book attractions in advance, metro is easy.

chengdu.label: Chengdu
chengdu.desc: #1 in central-west China. Must-see: Panda Base, Kuanzhai Alley, Jinli. Food: hotpot, chuanchuan, dandan noodles. Shop: panda brocade crafts. Tip: locals value "anyi" (comfort), take it slow.

chongqing.label: Chongqing
chongqing.desc: Fastest growing (+30%). Must-see: Hongyadong, Ciqikou, Yangtze cableway. Food: hotpot, xiaomian noodles, Jianghu cuisine. Shop: Jiefangbei. Tip: wear flats, GPS may fail in 8D terrain.

fuzhou.label: Fuzhou
fuzhou.desc: Coastal nature & heritage. Must-see: Sanfang Qixiang, Gushan, West Lake Park. Food: Buddha Jumps Over the Wall, fish balls, rouyan. Tip: abundant hot springs, wellness destination.

guangzhou.label: Guangzhou
guangzhou.desc: #1 in inbound spending. Must-see: Canton Tower, Shamian Island, Baiyun Mountain. Food: dim sum, roast meats, claypot rice. Shop: Tianhe malls, Baima clothing market. Tip: dense SE Asia flights, popular first stop.

guiyang.label: Guiyang
guiyang.desc: Korean "Seoul alternative", Western veggie haven. Must-see: Qianling Mountain, Qingyan Old Town, Huaxi. Food: sour soup fish, changwang noodles, siwawa wraps. Tip: cool summer escape.

hangzhou.label: Hangzhou
hangzhou.desc: Paradise on earth. Must-see: West Lake, Lingyin Temple, Longjing tea fields. Food: Dongpo pork, West Lake fish, Longjing shrimp. Shop: silk market, Southern Song Street. Tip: best in spring, avoid Golden Week.

harbin.label: Harbin
harbin.desc: SE Asia favorite. Must-see: Ice Festival, Zhongyang Street, St. Sophia. Food: guobaorou, red sausage, Russian cuisine. Shop: Russian imports on Zhongyang. Tip: -20°C in winter, bundle up.

kunming.label: Kunming
kunming.desc: Eternal spring. Must-see: Stone Forest, Dian Lake, Dounan flower market. Food: cross-bridge noodles, steam pot chicken, mushroom hotpot. Shop: fresh flowers, ethnic crafts. Tip: SE Asia gateway, pleasant year-round.

qingdao.label: Qingdao
qingdao.desc: Coastal gem. Must-see: Zhanqiao Pier, Badaguan, Laoshan. Food: seafood, Tsingtao beer, mackerel dumplings. Shop: Taidong pedestrian street. Tip: Beer Festival Jul-Aug, best in summer.

shanghai.label: Shanghai
shanghai.desc: 8.28M+ annual arrivals. Must-see: the Bund, Yu Garden, Disney. Food: xiaolongbao, shengjian, Shanghainese cuisine. Shop: Nanjing Rd, fabric market qipao. Tip: extensive metro, 72h transit visa usable.

shenzhen.label: Shenzhen
shenzhen.desc: China's Silicon Valley. Must-see: Huaqiangbei, Shenzhen Bay Park, OCT. Food: Chaoshan beef hotpot, seafood. Shop: Huaqiangbei electronics, MixC mall. Tip: 15min HSR to Hong Kong.

taiyuan.label: Taiyuan
taiyuan.desc: Black Myth: Wukong filming locations. Must-see: Jinci Temple, Twin Pagodas, Pingyao (nearby). Food: daoxiaomian noodles, guoyourou. Tip: follow the game trail, ancient architecture paradise.

tianjin.label: Tianjin
tianjin.desc: Port city with Italian concession. Must-see: Five Avenues, Ancient Culture Street, Eye of Tianjin. Food: goubuli buns, jianbing, mahua twists. Shop: Italian-style district. Tip: 30min HSR from Beijing.

hainan.label: Hainan
hainan.desc: China's Hawaii. Must-see: Yalong Bay, Wuzhizhou Island, Nanshan Temple. Food: Wenchang chicken, coconut rice. Shop: duty-free paradise. Tip: visa-free entry, tropical climate year-round.

xian.label: Xi'an
xian.desc: Ancient capital of 13 dynasties. Must-see: Terracotta Warriors, Ancient City Wall, Muslim Quarter. Food: roujiamo, yangrou paomo, biangbiang noodles. Shop: calligraphy, replicas. Tip: book Terracotta Warriors in advance.

yiwu.label: Yiwu
yiwu.desc: "World Small Commodity Capital". Flights +60% H1 2026. Must-visit: International Trade City (5 districts). Food: many Middle Eastern restaurants. Shop: phone cases to drones, one-stop.

zhengzhou.label: Zhengzhou
zhengzhou.desc: Hanfu street photography hotspot. Must-see: Erqi Tower, Henan Museum, Shaolin Temple (nearby). Food: huimian noodles, hulatang soup. Tip: wear traditional dress for photos, immersive Chinese aesthetic.

beijing.name: Beijing·Wangfujing
beijing.desc: Flagship electronics stores, heritage brands+modern malls. Same-day glasses. Dense tax refund stores with instant refund.

sanya.name: Sanya·Duty Free Mall
sanya.desc: Offshore & departure duty-free. Full range of intl brands. Policy upgraded 2025. Simple process: register→scan→pay→airport pickup, <3min.

shanghai.name: Shanghai·Fabric Market
shanghai.desc: South Bund Fabric Market etc. Custom qipao & suits, measured on-site. ¥300-2,000+, 2-3 day turnaround. Intl cards accepted.

shenzhen.name: Shenzhen·Huaqiangbei
shenzhen.desc: "China Electronics Street",35 specialized markets. Categories: phones, accessories, drones, wearables. Multilingual signs+intl cards+tax refund. Metro Line 1 Huaqiang Rd. Tip: compare prices, some markets negotiable.

yiwu.name: Yiwu·Trade City
yiwu.desc: "World Supermarket", phone cases to smart appliances to drones. Flight bookings +60% H1 2026. One-stop shopping, multilingual+intl cards.

jiahui.label: Shanghai Jiahui Intl Hospital
jiahui.desc: JCI-standard, multilingual team. Surgical (soft tissue, abdominoplasty, body contouring) & non-surgical (Botox, fillers). 10+ intl surgical cases/year.

mhwd.label: MHWD·Dingxiang Clinic
mhwd.desc: International dept, multilingual. All services under proper medical licensing with compliant equipment. Led by dermatology & injection experts.

rodeo.label: RODEO (Shanghai)
rodeo.desc: American cross-border chain. US-trained physicians with international brand certifications. Language-matched service staff, internationally compliant protocols.

yuansong.label: YuanSong Aesthetics (Shanghai)
yuansong.desc: Served clients from Korea, Japan, Middle East. Uses filler-based contouring instead of high-risk bone shaving. Natural aesthetics philosophy.

free.title: ✅ No Visa (Unilateral)
free.ruWarning: ⚠️ Russian citizens: Valid until Sep 14,2026 (not Dec 31)

transit.title: ✅ 240h Transit
transit.body: 55 countries. Onward ticket→≤240h(10d),24 provincial regions,60+ ports.

visaRequired.title: 📋 Visa Required
visaRequired.body: Apply in advance per nationality/purpose/duration. Visit local CVASC.

meta.title: Status Transfer
meta.sub: Already in China on a tourist/student visa? Here are your pathways to a long-term work/residence permit.

l1.intro: Choose your conversion pathway. Each has different requirements — tap to see the full step-by-step process.

pathA.label: Tourist → Work
pathA.title: Tourist → Work
steps: On a tourist visa (L) or visa-free entry. Get a job offer → employer sponsors work permit → convert to residence permit.
Receive a job offer from a Chinese employer willing to sponsor your work permit.
Employer applies for your Foreigner's Work Permit Notification Letter online (www.12333.gov.cn). Processing: ~6 business days average.
If your current visa allows in-country conversion: apply directly at the local Exit-Entry Bureau. Check with the bureau first — rules vary by city.
If in-country conversion is NOT allowed: you must exit China (e.g. to Hong Kong, Seoul, Bangkok), apply for a Z visa at a Chinese embassy, and re-enter.
Within 30 days of receiving your work permit: apply for a work-type Residence Permit at the PSB Exit-Entry Administration.
Complete medical check at a designated hospital (required for residence permit >1 year).
💡 Shanghai, Beijing, and GZ are generally more flexible with in-country conversion. Always call 12367 to confirm before making travel plans.

pathB.label: Student → Work
pathB.title: Student → Work
steps: For fresh graduates from Chinese universities. No need to exit China — convert directly in-country.
Graduate from a Chinese university (bachelor's, master's, or PhD).
Secure a job offer from a Chinese employer. The employer applies for your work permit.
You can convert your student residence permit (X1) to a work residence permit without leaving China.
Required documents: diploma (authenticated by CSCSE), employment contract, employer business license, health check, passport, and police registration.
Apply at the local Exit-Entry Bureau. Standard processing: 7-15 business days.
Note: this pathway is only for fresh graduates. If you graduated >1 year ago, use Path A instead.
💡 Many cities offer fast-track processing for foreign graduates of local universities. Shanghai and Beijing have dedicated 'foreign graduate talent' windows.

pathC.label: K Visa (STEM Talent)
pathC.title: K Visa (STEM Talent)
steps: Launched Oct 2025. Self-sponsored visa for STEM graduates. No employer needed.
Eligibility: STEM degree (bachelor's or above) from a recognized university, generally under ~40 years old.
No employer sponsorship required — self-apply. This is the biggest difference from Z visa.
Apply at a Chinese embassy/consulate abroad (or CVASC). Documents: passport, diploma + apostille + certified Chinese translation, CV, application form, photo.
Standard processing: 4-5 business days. Fee: $30-$140 depending on nationality. Total preparation time: ~3-4 weeks.
Visa features: 5-year validity, multiple entry, up to 180 days per stay. Permits work, study, research, business, and entrepreneurship — all on one visa.
After arrival: employer obtains work permit → you apply for residence permit within 30 days → register address within 24h.
Path to Green Card: Work 3 continuous years + reside ≥6 months/year + pay tax 3 years + income ~2-3× local avg. Total timeline: ~3.5-4 years.
💡 K visa is ideal if you want flexibility — you can freelance, change jobs, or start a company without restarting the visa process. Not for non-STEM fields (finance, marketing, law).

meta.title: Work & Residence
meta.sub: Two essential documents. New: one-stop online application, ~6 working days average.

l2.intro: The two-step process every foreign worker goes through. Since late 2025, a one-stop online portal integrates work permit, residence permit, and social security registration.

workPermit.label: Work Permit
workPermit.title: Foreigner's Work Permit
steps: Employer applies online. One-stop portal integrates work permit, residence permit, and social security since late 2025.
Employer registers at www.12333.gov.cn (select 'Corporate User'). Verification: 3-5 business days.

Prepare documents: passport (6+ months valid, 2+ blank pages), degree certificate (apostille + certified Chinese translation), no-criminal-record certificate (<6 months old, apostille), health check (from designated facility), 2-inch white-background photo, CV (past 10 years, no gaps), employment contract.

Points-based classification: Category A (85+ points, high-end talent, 5-year permit), Category B (60-84, professional, 1-3 years), Category C (<60, general worker, quota-limited). Points from: salary, education, experience, age, Chinese language.

Submit application through the one-stop portal. Three departments review in parallel: Science & Technology Bureau (qualifications), Exit-Entry Administration (passport/visa history), Human Resources Bureau (social security).
Processing time: 3-15 business days, ~6 days national average. Shanghai: ~3 days. Newer cities: 10-15 days.
Receive Notification Letter (PDF). Use this to apply for Z visa or convert in-country.

⏱ Total: 6-12 weeks (including 4-8 weeks for document authentication). Start 3 months before your intended start date. — 💡 As of Dec 2024, the physical work permit card is eliminated. Work permit info is embedded in the electronic 'Join in Card' via the 12333 APP.

residencePermit.label: Residence Permit
residencePermit.title: Residence Permit
steps: Apply within 30 days of entry. This is a sticker in your passport — it's your long-term stay authorization.
Apply at the local Public Security Bureau (PSB) Exit-Entry Administration.

Required documents: passport (with valid visa + entry stamp), Notification of Foreigner's Work Permit (printed), completed application form, recent 2-inch photo (white background, 48×33mm), police registration proof (from hotel or police station), health check certificate (for permits >1 year), employer business license copy (first-time only), employment contract.
Submit all documents. Your passport will be held during processing — you'll receive a receipt that serves as proof of status.
Processing: 5-10 business days. Collect your passport with the residence permit sticker affixed.
Validity: typically 1 year for first-timers, 1-5 years for renewals. Category A talent may get 5 years on first application.
Fees: ¥400 (<1 year), ¥800 (1-3 years), ¥1,000 (3-5 years).
⏱ Processing: 5-10 business days. Apply within 30 days of entry. — 💡 The residence permit replaces your visa. Once you have it, you can enter/exit China freely during its validity period.

meta.title: Phone + Bank
meta.sub: Chinese phone number + bank account = full digital life. Unlock all app features.

l3.intro: A Chinese phone number and bank account are the keys to everyday life in China — from mobile payment to food delivery to ride-hailing. Get these two sorted first.

phone.label: SIM Card
phone.title: Chinese Phone Number
steps: Visit a China Mobile / China Unicom / China Telecom service hall with your original passport (not a copy).
Choose a plan: ¥29-99/month. Includes data, domestic calls + SMS. Ask for English service at larger branches.
The SIM is tied to your passport. Keep your passport with you — you'll need it for account changes.
Why it matters: Many apps (Alipay, WeChat, DiDi, Meituan) require a Chinese number for full functionality. Foreign numbers work but have limited features.
Keep your home country SIM for 2FA verification codes from your home bank/services.
Pro tip: Some carriers offer tourist eSIMs for short stays. For long-term, get a physical SIM for reliable service.

bank.label: Bank Account
bank.title: Chinese Bank Account
steps: Visit a major bank branch: Bank of China (BOC), ICBC, China Merchants Bank, or China Construction Bank. ICBC has the most English-friendly branches.
Required documents: original passport, residence permit (or visa + registration form), work permit (or employment contract), Chinese phone number.
Open a Class II account initially (limited to ¥10,000/day). Upgrade to Class I (unlimited) once you have a residence permit and work permit.
Get a UnionPay debit card. Link it to Alipay and WeChat Pay immediately at the counter (staff can help).
This lifts the ¥200 transaction limit on Alipay/WeChat Pay. You can now send/receive large amounts.
Enable mobile banking app. Set up cross-border transfer if you need to send money home (Wise, PayPal, or SWIFT via bank app).
Credit cards: Difficult for first-year foreigners. Use your home country credit card + Chinese debit card as a pair.

l3.tip: 💡 Priority order: Get SIM → open bank account → link to Alipay/WeChat. Without a Chinese phone number, you cannot open a bank account. Without a bank account, your mobile payment features are limited.

meta.title: Housing
meta.sub: Where to live, how to rent, and mandatory registration. 7 cities now support online registration.

l4.intro: Ziroom and Beike are the two biggest rental platforms. Standard contract is 1 month deposit + 3 months rent. Police registration is mandatory within 24 hours of moving in.
l4.sinceMar2026: 💡 Since Mar 202612367 platform supports online registration in 7 pilot cities: Shanghai, Beijing, Guangzhou, Shenzhen, Chengdu, Hangzhou, Xiamen.

rent.label: Renting
rent.title: Rental Guide
steps: Ziroom (自如) and Beike (贝壳) are the two biggest rental platforms. Both have some English support.
🏢 Platforms: Ziroom (自如) — standardized apartments, English app, quick maintenance. Beike (贝壳) — largest listing platform, wider range. Lianjia (链家) — major brokerage, physical offices everywhere.

📝 Contract norms: Standard is '押一付三' (1 month deposit + 3 months rent upfront). Always get a bilingual contract (Chinese + English). Confirm in writing that the landlord will assist with police registration — if they refuse, find another apartment.

📍 Expat neighborhoods: Shanghai — Gubei, Jing'an, French Concession. Beijing — Chaoyang (Sanlitun), Shunyi (villas, family-friendly), Liangmaqiao. Guangzhou — Tianhe, Zhujiang New Town. Shenzhen — Shekou, Nanshan.
⚠️ Red flags: Landlord refuses to provide property ownership certificate (needed for registration). No written contract. Asking for >3 months rent upfront. No heat in winter (northern cities).
💰 Cost reference (mid-2026): Shanghai/Jing'an 1BR: ¥8K-25K/month. Beijing/Chaoyang: ¥7K-20K. GZ/Tianhe: ¥4K-12K. SZ/Shekou: ¥6K-15K.

registration.label: Registration
registration.title: Police Registration
steps: Mandatory within 24 hours of moving to a new address. Legal basis: Exit-Entry Administration Law Art. 39.
Hotel residents: The hotel registers you automatically at check-in — just hand over your passport. No action needed.

Private residence: Go to the local police station (派出所) within 24 hours of moving in. Bring: passport, residence permit (or visa), rental contract, landlord's property ownership certificate copy, landlord's ID copy.
Online registration (7 pilot cities): Shanghai, Beijing, Guangzhou, Shenzhen, Chengdu, Hangzhou, Xiamen. Use NIA website (s.nia.gov.cn) or 12367 APP. Since March 2026.
Re-register every time you move. This is a legal requirement, not optional. Your registration receipt is needed for residence permit renewal.
Failure to register: Warning on first offense. Repeated failure: fine up to ¥2,000 (Art. 76). Can affect future visa/residence permit applications.
Pro tip: Keep a photo of your registration receipt on your phone. You'll need it for bank, phone, and permit applications.
💡 Since Mar 2026, the 12367 platform supports online registration in 7 pilot cities. This is a game-changer — no more queuing at the police station.

meta.title: Insurance
meta.sub: Five mandatory insurances + housing fund. Most expats also buy private international coverage.

l5.intro: Foreign employees must enroll in all five social insurances within 30 days of work permit issuance. Employer contributions add ~26-37% on top of your gross salary. Most expats also buy supplementary international health insurance.

socialIns.label: Social Insurance
socialIns.title: Five Social Insurances + Housing Fund
steps: Mandatory for all foreign employees. Registration must be completed within 30 days of work permit issuance. Contribution rates match Chinese employees.
🏦 Pension (Employee 8% + Employer 14-16%): You can withdraw your personal account balance (8% + interest) when permanently leaving China. Employer contributions stay in the pooling fund.
🏥 Medical (Employee 2% + Employer ~6.85-10%): Covers public hospital visits with partial reimbursement. Most expats use this as a base and add private insurance for international clinics.
📋 Unemployment (Employee 0.2-0.5% + Employer 0.5-0.8%): Provides limited benefits. Must have contributed 1+ years and be 'involuntarily unemployed.'
⚠️ Work Injury (Employer 0.16-1.9%, Employee 0%): Covers work-related injuries and occupational diseases. Employer pays full cost.
👶 Maternity (merged with Medical): Covers childbirth costs and maternity leave pay. Both male and female employees contribute.

🏠 Housing Fund (Employee 5-12% + Employer 5-12%): Varies by city. Shanghai allows foreigners to opt out. Your contributions + employer match go into a personal account. Can be withdrawn when leaving China or buying property.

🌐 Bilateral exemptions: 12 countries (DE, KR, DK, FI, CA, CH, NL, ES, LU, JP, RS, KG) have social security agreements with China — may exempt pension/unemployment contributions. Medical + work injury still mandatory. Apply for Certificate of Coverage.
💡 Total employer cost above your gross salary: ~26-37%. This is part of your total compensation — negotiate with this in mind.

privateIns.label: Private Insurance
privateIns.title: International Health Insurance
steps: Most expats buy supplementary international insurance for access to private clinics with English-speaking doctors.
🏥 Major providers: Cigna (~¥15K-30K/yr), Allianz (~¥12K-25K/yr), Bupa (~¥20K-50K/yr), Aetna, MSH China. All have direct billing with international hospitals.
🏥 Key hospitals: Shanghai — Jiahui, UFH, Parkway Health. Beijing — UFH, PUMCH International, OASIS. Guangzhou — Clifford, CanAm. Shenzhen — UFH Shekou.
💰 Cost reference: GP visit ¥800-1,500 at international dept, specialist ¥1,200-2,500. ER visit ¥2,000-5,000. Hospitalization ¥5,000-15,000/night. Insurance is strongly recommended.
💡 Some employers provide Cigna/Allianz as part of the expat package. If not, buy individual coverage — one hospital visit without insurance can cost more than a year's premium.

meta.title: Drive + School
meta.sub: Driver's license conversion and international school options for expat families.

l6.kicker: Hit the Road, Hit the Books
l6.intro: Foreign licenses aren't valid in China — but conversion is straightforward: temporary permit, direct swap, or written test. For families, international schools offer US/UK/IB curricula. Plan 6-12 months ahead.

license.label: Driver's License
license.title: Driver's License
steps: Foreign licenses are not valid in China. You have three options: temporary permit, direct swap (some countries), or take the written test.

📋 Temporary Permit: Valid 3 months. Apply at the vehicle management office (车管所) with: passport, foreign license, notarized Chinese translation, residence registration, 3 photos, health check. Processing: same day to 3 days. Good for short-term needs.

🔄 Direct Swap: Citizens of Belgium, France (and a few others) can directly exchange their license for a Chinese one — no test required. Check with your country's embassy for the latest list, as bilateral agreements change.

📝 Written Test (most foreigners): Pass the 100-question multiple-choice test (90/100 to pass). Available in English, Japanese, Korean, Russian, French, German, Spanish, and more. Study the official app (驾考宝典). Book at your local vehicle management office. After passing: receive a Chinese C1 license (valid 6 years).

📍 Where to apply: Vehicle Management Office (车辆管理所). Bring: passport + residence permit, foreign license + notarized translation, residence registration form, health check (from designated hospital), 3-5 photos.
💡 Unlike many countries, China does not require a driving test (road test) for foreign license conversion — just the written test. The test focuses on Chinese traffic rules and signs.
license.sub: Temp permit, direct swap, or written test

school.label: Schools
school.title: International Schools
steps: Three types: pure international (foreign passport only), bilingual (mixed), and public school international departments. Plan 6-12 months ahead.

🏫 School types:
1) Pure International: Foreign passport only. Follow US/UK/IB curriculum. English instruction. Best fit if planning to return home for university.
2) Bilingual: Chinese + English. Open to both foreign and Chinese students. More immersion in local culture.
3) Public School Intl Dept: Attached to top public schools. Chinese curriculum + international elements. Most affordable.
💰 Annual tuition (2025-2026): Pure International: ¥150K-350K+. Bilingual: ¥80K-200K. Public Intl Dept: ¥30K-80K. Plus: application fee (¥2K-5K), uniform, meals, bus, activities.
📍 Key areas: Beijing — Shunyi (ISB, WAB, Dulwich, BSY). Shanghai — Pudong (Dulwich, Wellington, Concordia), Changning (YCIS, SCIS). Guangzhou — Tianhe, Panyu. Shenzhen — Shekou (SIS, BASIS).

📋 Admission: Apply 6-12 months before start date. Most schools require: previous school reports, recommendation letters, student interview, parent interview, English proficiency assessment. Some have waitlists of 1-2 years.

💡 Pure international schools legally cannot admit Chinese passport holders (except Hong Kong/Macau/Taiwan). If your child holds a Chinese passport, look at bilingual schools or public school international departments.

meta.title: Daily Life
meta.sub: Shopping, eating, and dressing for life in China. Same-day delivery, 24/7 food delivery, and four distinct seasons.

l7.intro: Once you have your phone, bank, and apartment sorted, here's everything you need for daily life — from where to shop to what to eat to how to dress.

shopping.label: Shopping
shopping.title: Online Shopping & Groceries
steps: Taobao (淘宝): The biggest marketplace — everything from furniture to phone cases. Prices are unbeatable. Use image search to find items. Most sellers ship same/next day.
JD (京东): Electronics, appliances, and branded goods. Better quality control, higher prices. JD Logistics delivers within hours in major cities. Recommended for electronics.
Pinduoduo (拼多多): Budget platform. Great for daily essentials and household items. Group-buy model for lowest prices.
Hema (盒马): Alibaba's fresh grocery chain. 30-minute delivery to your door. Great for imported foods, fresh produce, and ready-to-eat meals. Higher prices but excellent quality.
Sam's Club / Costco: Both in major cities. Bulk imported goods. Great for cheese, wine, and Western staples.
Import supermarkets: CityShop (城市超市), Ole', BHG — stock Western/Japanese/Korean groceries. Pricey but worth it for comfort food.

food.label: Dining
food.title: Eating Out & Delivery
steps: Dianping (大众点评): The Yelp of China. Restaurant reviews, photos, ratings. Search by cuisine type, area, or price. Essential for finding good restaurants.
Meituan (美团) / Ele.me (饿了么): Food delivery to your door, typically 30-40 minutes. Coverage is nearly universal. Delivery fee ¥3-8. Both built into Alipay and WeChat.
WeChat translate: Point your phone camera at a Chinese menu → WeChat scan → translate. Not perfect, but good enough to understand what you're ordering.

Dietary needs: Halal — look for 清真 (qīngzhēn) signs or Lanzhou beef noodle chains. Vegetarian — say 纯素 (chún sù) for pure vegan, 素食 (sù shí) for vegetarian. Watch for meat broth in vegetable dishes. Allergies — carry a bilingual allergy card.
Paying: Almost no one uses cash. Scan the table QR code with Alipay/WeChat to order AND pay. Some restaurants are fully QR-code ordering — no physical menu.
Tip culture: No tipping in China. It's not expected and sometimes refused.

clothing.label: Clothing
clothing.title: Sizing & Seasonal Dressing
steps: Sizing: China sizes are 1-2 smaller than US/EU. US M = CN L/XL. US L = CN XXL. Always check the size chart in cm — don't rely on letter sizes. Uniqlo and Zara in China use global sizing, so they're reliable references.
Spring (Mar-May, 10-22°C): Light jacket + layers. This is the most comfortable season. Occasional rain in the south — bring a light raincoat.

Summer (Jun-Aug, 28-38°C+): Hot and humid, especially south of the Yangtze. Breathable fabrics (cotton, linen). Sunscreen, hat, and UV umbrella essential. Every building has AC. Carry a light cardigan for indoor cold.
Autumn (Sep-Nov, 10-25°C): The BEST season. Clear skies, comfortable temperatures. Light sweater or jacket in the evening. Ideal for travel and outdoor activities.

Winter (Dec-Feb): North (Beijing/Harbin): -10°C to -20°C. Heavy down coat, thermal layers, gloves, hat, boots — you need full winter gear. South (Shanghai/Guangzhou): 0-10°C. Damp cold feels colder than the number. Layers work better than one thick coat. Indoor heating is universal in the north, but southern apartments may not have central heating — check before renting.

transport.label: Transport
transport.title: Getting Around Daily
steps: Metro: Best way to get around major cities. Use Alipay transport QR code at gates — no ticket needed. ¥2-10 per ride. Extensive networks in all Tier 1/2 cities. English signs and announcements in most cities.
DiDi (滴滴): Built into Alipay and WeChat. English UI available. Pay with linked foreign or Chinese card. Multiple car types (Express, Premier, Taxi). ~¥20-50 for a typical city ride.
Shared bikes: Scan with Alipay/Meituan. ¥1.5-3 per ride. No deposit for most. Hello Bike (哈啰), Meituan Bike (美团单车), DiDi Bike (青桔). Blue, yellow, green — you'll recognize them.
High-Speed Rail (高铁): For inter-city travel. Book at 12306.cn (English version available). Foreign passports accepted. 300+ km/h. Shanghai to Beijing: 4.5 hours. Book 1-2 weeks ahead for peak times.
Ride-hailing alternatives: CaoCao (曹操出行) — premium cars. T3 (T3出行) — backed by state automakers. Both have English support in some cities.

meta.title: Compliance
meta.sub: Renewal reminders, overstay consequences, permanent residence pathway, and emergency contacts.

l8.intro: Stay on top of renewal deadlines and know who to call in an emergency. Overstay penalties can be severe — set a calendar reminder 60 days before your permit expires.

renewal.label: Renewal & Compliance
renewal.title: Staying Legal
steps: Your residence permit must be renewed 30 days before expiry. Overstaying has serious consequences.
Renew 30-60 days before expiry. Set a calendar reminder. Late renewal may require explanation and additional documents.
Overstay penalties: ¥500 per day of overstay, up to ¥10,000 maximum. In serious cases: 5-15 days detention + deportation. A 5-year entry ban may be imposed.

Lost/stolen passport: 1) File a police report immediately (call 110 or visit the nearest police station). 2) Get a police report receipt. 3) Contact your embassy/consulate for an emergency travel document or new passport. 4) Transfer your residence permit to the new passport within 10 days at the Exit-Entry Bureau.

Change of employer: New employer must apply for a new work permit. Then update your residence permit at the Exit-Entry Bureau within 10 days of the change. You cannot start working for the new employer until both permits are updated.
Change of address: Re-register at the new police station within 24 hours of moving. Update your address in the Exit-Entry system.
Lost residence permit: Report to the Exit-Entry Bureau immediately. Apply for a replacement (¥200 fee, 7-10 business days).

🟢 Permanent Residence (Green Card): Eligibility — work 3+ continuous years in China, reside ≥6 months/year, pay individual tax for 3 consecutive years, income ~2-6× local average (~¥240K-720K/year). K visa pathway: ~2-3× local avg. Apply via Exit-Entry Bureau. Processing: 6-12 months, quota applies. Green card holders can work without a separate permit and enter/exit China freely.

⚠️ Critical: Never let your residence permit expire. Overstay records can permanently affect future visa applications. If you realize it's expiring and you can't renew in time, call 12367 for guidance BEFORE it expires.

emergency.label: Emergency
emergency.title: Emergency Contacts
emergency.desc: Save these numbers. Tap each for details.
emergency.passportWarningDetail: 1) File police report immediately (call 110). 2) Contact your embassy for emergency travel document. 3) Keep a photo of your passport and visa on your phone. 4) Transfer residence permit to new passport within 10 days.
